cause_insufficiency.reservation: An alternative or additional sufficient explanation is simply normal methodological sequencing (cheap pilot before an expensive round), independent of any resource constraint; not ruled out here.

additional_cause.reservation: A rival, non-exclusive explanation: benchmark designers may simply have prioritized commercially/security-salient state-changing (tool_action) attacks independent of any register theory, rather than the absence of a register field being the operative constraint; both could be true simultaneously.

clarity.reservation: Modeling convention: this edge represents TRIM-1 *neutralizing* NBR-1 (per FRT-006's reachability-from-a-trimming-injection contract), not TRIM-1 producing NBR-1 as an effect.
Same convention as CLM-8: TRIM-2 neutralizes NBR-2 by requiring a held-out human-calibration batch before trusting judge-only numbers.

conflict_claims.statement: D (keep Identity, Evaluation, and Authority permanently separate) and D' (merge any pair that tests low-kappa) cannot both be fully honored for the same pair: honoring D unconditionally forecloses ever merging on evidence, while honoring D' as a live policy means the taxonomy's final shape is not yet fixed and could still collapse the very distinctions Need B wants preserved.

alternative_candidates.rejected_because: A bigger corpus alone doesn't fix same-model-family/single-coder authorship; Study 2's caveat is about WHO labels, not how many cases exist (see hypothesize's own LIM-SEED-CORPUS-UNDERSIZED limitation).
RCPI's own scorer capability is already demonstrably satisfied (CAP-SCORE-DETERMINISTIC etc. all implemented and tested); adding scorer features doesn't touch the independence gap.

constraint_assessment.exploit_direction: Sequence the cheapest, fully in-project-control prerequisite first (enrich the corpus with boundary cases, IO-1) so that when independent labeling/judging capacity is secured, it is spent on a corpus that can actually make the taxonomy lose, rather than re-running the same easy exemplars.
constraint_assessment.limiting_mechanism: Every downstream empirical claim in research/portfolio.toml (HYP-COVERAGE-CONCENTRATION, HYP-TAXONOMY-REPRODUCIBILITY, HYP-CANDIDATE-MERGES-DISTINCT, HYP-DISSOCIATION, HYP-ARCHITECTURE-REDUCES-CAPTURES) currently funnels through the same single missing capability -- independent, non-author, non-same-model- family evidence production -- so no other improvement (a bigger corpus alone, a faster scorer, a second harness adapter) raises the count of non-quarantined conclusions until this exists.

contradictions: manual/hostile-uptake-manual.md.pdf section 7.2's capture-depth/severity table presents "silent conflict-suppression" as its own 8th severity rung ("critical"), while tool/rcpi/rcpi/metrics.py and tool/rcpi/rcpi/corpus/definitions.md section 3 model silence as an orthogonal second axis (a boolean disclosed flag) joined with capture depth to form silent_critical_capture_rate, not an additional depth level. Adopt a strict per-class-kappa arbitration policy: commit in advance to merge any registers.CANDIDATE_MERGES pair whose independently-labeled per-class kappa on a boundary-enriched corpus falls below an explicit, preregistered threshold, and to keep it separate otherwise -- so the decision is never made by prior theoretical commitment (Need B) alone, but always by the Need-C evidence standard.

Build or adopt a cross-model-family disclosure judge with an explicit independence guard (refusing to judge when the judge and subject model families match), following the pattern already prototyped off-branch.

Recruit at least 2-3 labelers who did not author any RCPI case or attack (genuine humans, or at minimum a model family distinct from both the case-authoring process and each other) to re-run Study 2's protocol on a boundary-case-enriched corpus.
The taxonomy's final register set is determined by evidence (per-class kappa on boundary-enriched, independently-labeled data) rather than by an a priori commitment to richness.
A real, valid silent-capture number can be computed from real model transcripts.
HYP-DISSOCIATION becomes empirically testable for the first time on this branch, rather than remaining mechanism-only.
Independent, non-author labels exist on a boundary-case-enriched corpus, enabling a real crispness test rather than a same-model pilot.

The kappa-arbitration policy (INJ-1) is applied using another same-model-family pilot, silently re-producing the independence gap (NC-2) it was meant to fix, and a class gets wrongly merged or wrongly kept.

Gate INJ-1's kappa-arbitration decision on NC-2 (independent, non-same-model-family raters) being satisfied first; refuse to treat kappa computed from same-model-family raters as arbitration-grade evidence, mirroring hypothesize's own maturity gate that keeps such evidence at "mechanism" and never lets it move a conclusion.

A cross-model-family judge (INJ-2) is wired in, but its agreement with a genuine human standard is never checked, so independent_judge provenance is silently treated as equivalent to human labeling when hypothesize's own maturity ladder distinguishes internal_pilot from comparative_pilot and external_replication.

Require at least one held-out, human-labeled comparison batch to calibrate the cross-family judge against human labels before trusting judge-only silent-capture numbers at internal_pilot maturity or above.
The current seed corpus (21 cases) has no boundary cases deliberately stressing the CANDIDATE_MERGES pairs or the instruction<->tool_action wobble Study 2 surfaced.
No recruiting channel or protocol exists yet for engaging labelers who are not the repository's own authoring process, whether human contractors or a genuinely separate model family.

The existing blinding tooling (make_sheet.py / keymap.json) was built and exercised only for the same-model pilot; it has not been checked against a larger, boundary-case-enriched corpus or a genuinely external rater pool's practical needs.

An expanded corpus exists with at least two deliberately ambiguous boundary cases per flagged register pair (identity<->authority, evaluation<->authority) and for the read-only-disclosure-vs-send- exfiltration wobble, alongside the existing 21 seed items.
A concrete recruiting/engagement plan (who, how many, compensation or equivalent, timeline) for at least two non-author labelers is written down and actionable.
The blinding pipeline and protocol.md are re-verified, or lightly adapted, to work for the enlarged corpus and for a human (not only an agent) audience.
tool/rcpi/rcpi/corpus/seed_cases.jsonl has 21 cases (15 attacks + 6 controls) with no items deliberately designed to stress a flagged register-merge boundary.
Need at least two boundary-case items per flagged merge pair so the taxonomy can actually lose, per definitions.md's own "letting it lose" discipline.

Author and append boundary-case items to tool/rcpi/rcpi/corpus/seed_cases.jsonl for the identity<->authority, evaluation<->authority, and instruction<->tool_action (read-only disclosure) wobbles, each with an author_provisional_label and a null gold_label, following the existing Case schema.
seed_cases.jsonl contains the new boundary items and `rcpi validate` reports them well-formed against a companion runs file covering the new ids.
No recruiting/engagement plan for non-author labelers exists.
Need a concrete plan to actually engage at least two independent labelers.
Draft a short recruiting/engagement note (who to approach, what they would do, how blinding works for them, expected time cost) as a new docs file, e.g. studies/study2_interrater/HUMAN_ROUND_PLAN.md.
A committed plan document exists that names candidate labelers or channels and a concrete protocol.

open_questions: Is RC-1 (a resource/access gap) the right explanation for why independent evidence hasn't been produced yet, or is it simply normal pilot-then-scale sequencing regardless of resource constraints? The repository does not state this explicitly either way (see CLM-1's open causality_existence / cause_insufficiency CLR checks).

Would the theoretical contribution of the signs-of-change framework survive intact if one or both CANDIDATE_MERGES pairs actually merge under real independent-rater testing (ASM-1)? The manual argues the richer register set is the paper's central addition (section 2), but does not say how much of that argument depends on keeping all seven registers distinct versus, say, five or six.

The manual's own severity ladder (section 7.2) lists "silent conflict-suppression" as an 8th rung alongside capture-depth levels 1-7, while the shipped code (metrics.py, definitions.md section 3) treats capture depth and disclosure as two strictly separate axes whose join produces the silent-critical-capture rate, not a literal 8th level. Which framing should govern future exposition has not been reconciled in the source documents themselves (see contradictions and EVD-7).

predicted_effects.statement: If RC-1 (a resource/access gap, not mere sequencing) explains the current gap, the study designs/protocols themselves should already be complete, with only resource-dependent execution (recruiting, running) outstanding.

If quarantine (not some other mechanism) is why current findings can't move a conclusion, a synthetic qualified+preregistered evidence item should escape quarantine and move a conclusion under direct engine invocation.

If the missing disclosure-labeling mechanism (RC-2) is a real blocking cause, the one adapter that exists (AgentDojo) should refuse to produce a valid result at exactly the disclosure step and nowhere else in its conversion pipeline.
If existing benchmarks' native metrics genuinely lack any register or disclosure field (RC-3), Study 1's own coded scores_silence field should read false for every source with no exception.

conflict_claims.statement: Building broad harness/simulated-arm breadth (D) and investing in a real, deterministic, resource-governed learned arm (D') draw on the same engineering attention and the same zero-dependency, deterministic-CI design constraint; adding real-arm infrastructure risks breaking the pure-Python, multi-version CI that D's harness depends on (NBR-1), so the two cannot both be fully pursued without first resolving that dependency tension.

alternative_candidates.rejected_because: CSF-3 itself cannot be the constraint entity (it is a critical_success_factor, not a constraint, per CON-001); its unsatisfied portion is the substance behind CONSTRAINT-1, but the specific limiting sub-mechanism is T3 evidential strength, not T1/S1-S4.
RC-1 explains why the harness was built breadth-first (a historical cause) but is not itself a present throughput limiter now that T1/T2 and mechanism pilots already run.

constraint_assessment.exploit_direction: Prioritize research/portfolio.toml ST-06 ('T3 schema-transfer experiment') from specified_inactive to running, since it is the only study whose criterion can move GH-LRN off not_tested; do not let further mechanism-building on adjacent capabilities (INJ-2/3/4) substitute for it.

goal_measure.name: Decision-grade P1-P6 hypothesis verdicts
goal_measure.unit: scientific hypotheses resolved supported/falsified/inconclusive (of those in research/portfolio.toml)

constraint_assessment.limiting_mechanism: No independently generated, population-backed T3 benchmark with real learned and e-graph systems under matched resource governance exists yet (research/portfolio.toml ST-06 is 'specified_inactive'); every other CSF (kernel trust, leakage/accounting, genuine T1/T2 systems, exact oracles, reproducibility, honest disclosure) already has working, exercised machinery. Building more mechanism elsewhere cannot move a hypothesis from not_tested toward supported/falsified/inconclusive; only running ST-06 can.

contradictions: CON-1: README frames the goal as the full confirmatory experiment (G-1), while docs/pilot-report.md concludes the evidence supports an assurance-layer path (G-alt-B). Not a defect — an unresolved strategic fork (OQ-1).

CON-2: README/pilot-report say "capture-causing proposals are rejected"; the graphist code makes capture impossible by construction (fresh re-minting) and rejects out-of-scope arguments, but no traced path yields a distinct "capture verdict". The claim is slightly looser than the code.

Several thin bgamma domain modules (render.py, validation.py, rules.py, diagram.py, certificate.py, name.py, signature.py, sorts.py) were confirmed exercised by tests but not read line-by-line.
The two source PDFs were confirmed by first page / corroborated by formal/ + README, not read in full.
Numeric experiment outcomes are as asserted by tests and by one live run each; not swept across many seeds.

Against: no real learned system participates yet, so the flagship (learned) form of the question is not currently answerable; docs/pilot-report.md reads the pilot as supporting an assurance-layer reading instead (EVD-4). entities.statement: Conclusions separate machine-checked kernel properties from empirical ML claims, and disclose simulation seams.
The experiment cannot produce a confirmatory verdict on the paper's flagship claim (that the representation improves *learned* reasoning), because no real learned system participates.
Any comparison involving S1–S4 is non-evidential — their correctness is a deterministic hash checked against a hardcoded accuracy table, so results encode the author's expectation, not a measurement.
No arm actually solves battery T1; T1 outcomes are produced by the scripted competence model, so T1 yields no comparative evidence.
Four of six ablations (A2/A3/A4/A6) are illustrative, so the "A1-A6 differ from S8 in exactly one component" claim (DoD #6) is unmet and P5 is forced inconclusive.

The readiness signal misleads — 20 of 21 features are @must including ones exercising simulated data, and no scenario carries a @wip or @skip tag, so a green CI ("all @must pass") reads as "confirmatory-ready" when it is not.

Assurance is inverted — the larger, science-bearing bgamma package has neither strict typing nor architecture/facade enforcement, while the smaller graphist slice has both; regressions are most likely where they are least guarded.

Parts of bgamma's reproducibility surface prove nothing — rebuild compares a function to itself in-process, and run-provenance values are hardcoded placeholders — so bgamma's reproducibility claim is weaker than graphist's demonstrated one.
The definition-of-done leakage check runs against a synthetic demo manifest, not the actual evaluation dataset, so "leak-free before unlock" is proven for the checker, not for the real items.
Dead/ambiguous scaffolding (ablation_service canned A3/A4/A6 numbers; humanaudit machinery never fed real data) could be misread as measurements if quoted out of context.
Breadth-before-real-depth sequencing — the full S1–S8 × T1–T5 comparison *surface* was built end-to-end with scripted stand-ins before even one real learned participant existed.

A deliberate zero-runtime-dependency, fully-deterministic design (dependencies = []) makes a real learned model expensive to add (ML stack, compute governance, nondeterminism), so simulation was the low-cost choice.
The near-term deliverable was chosen to be demonstrable trustworthiness/honesty of the *machinery*, explicitly not a learning claim; the project excelled at that and deferred the empirical substance.

Trusted-boundary discipline (strict mypy, architecture test) was scoped by package *name* ("graphist" = "trusted") rather than by where empirical risk actually lives (bgamma), leaving the risky package least guarded.

Introduce ONE real, deterministic, dependency-free learned arm that actually SOLVES real T2 (capture-free substitution) by proposing a transformation through the existing application port, verified by the existing real kernel and graded by the existing isomorphism oracle.

Split the readiness signal: distinguish "machinery-verified" from "science-measured", exclude simulated capabilities from any confirmatory gate, and surface the seams as a first-class, ordered realization backlog.
Extend strict mypy and the architecture/facade test to the bgamma package.
Make bgamma reproducibility real — out-of-process rebuild comparison, genuine run-provenance capture — and run the leakage check against the actual evaluation dataset.
A real learned arm could require heavy infra (torch/JAX, GPU, threads) that breaks the zero-dependency, deterministic-reproducibility design (threatening CSF-5) and the 3.11–3.13 pure-Python CI.

Constrain the first real arm to a small, CPU-trainable, seed-deterministic, pure-Python model — extend the existing pure-Python SGD lineage (bgamma/domain/learning/model.py) — behind the existing arm contract and resource-limited runner, capturing real training provenance (which also discharges part of INJ-4). No new runtime dependency.
There is no learned proposer in the arm contract for the propose->verify loop (only scripted or fixed-rule proposers).
A learned model must stay deterministic and reproducible to preserve the harness's core guarantee.
New heavy dependencies would break the zero-dep, multi-version pure-Python CI.
Training provenance is currently placeholder, so a trained arm could not be reproducibly attributed.
Choosing the first real task matters — T1 needs a not-yet-existing structured-query contract, whereas T2's oracle, kernel β, codecs and grading are already real.
A deterministic, pure-Python feature encoding of the T2 representation exists and is stable across runs.
A learned proposer conforms to the existing arm/runner contract and submits proposals through the application port for real kernel verification.
Real training provenance (data hashes, seeds, environment fingerprint) is recorded for the arm.
T2 is fixed as the first real-arm target (kernel/oracle/codec/grading all already real there).

Implement `LearnedProposerArm` (T2): encode the native-diagram/scope-graph representation into deterministic pure-Python features, train the existing SGD model on the real T2 train split to propose a capture-free substitution, submit it through the existing runner/application port so the real kernel verifies it and issues or rejects a certificate, grade with the existing isomorphism oracle, and keep every scheduled item accounted. Record real provenance.

Simulated arms cannot yield evidence about representation because their outputs are functions of a hardcoded table, not of the task.
A real learned arm necessarily costs materially more than a simulated one under this design.
Proving machinery trustworthy first was a prerequisite to trusting later real-arm results.
"graphist = trusted, bgamma = broad" led assurance tooling to follow the name, not the risk.
T1 and the S1–S4 comparisons are load-bearing for the flagship (learned) claim.

Evidential strength on T3 (schema/graph transfer) is the limiting mechanism for a decision-grade verdict on G-1: CSF-3 (genuine systems under comparison) is the associated critical success factor and remains unsatisfied, but the specific higher-leverage gap is the missing population-backed T3 study, not T1 (unsolved) or S1-S4 (simulated), which do not gate GH-LRN's decisive comparisons the way T3 does.
No learned proposer participates in the T1/T2 propose-verify loop; every arm is either a hardcoded lookup (S1-S4) or a scripted symbolic solver (S5-S8).
A genuine, deterministic, dependency-free learned proposer that submits real transformations through the existing verified port.
`pytest tests/` exercises a new LearnedProposerArm that emits kernel-verified certificates on held-out T2 items, replacing the SimulatedArm fallback for T2.
Produce credible, decision-grade evidence on the BΓ reasoning-advantage question.
Prove the comparison machinery is trustworthy and reproducible now.
Measure real learned systems so gaps reflect representation, not scripting.
Build the verified kernel, lock, codecs, and differential, and a full harness with simulated arms.
Invest in real arms and real T1 before calling the harness confirmatory.

additional_cause.reservation: This repository is substantially Lovable/gpt-engineer-app[bot] generated (EVD-8); an AI website-builder optimizing for a complete-looking investor demo is a plausible additional or alternative cause for choosing "always green" placeholders over an honest pending state. Not adjudicated here.

causality_existence.reservation: Only a hardcoded literal, or a role-only client check, can produce this observed effect without backend integration; this is an architectural necessity given the current code, not mere correlation.

cause_insufficiency.reservation: RC-1 alone (a general tendency toward UI mockups) would not by itself force a *fabricated pass*, and AC-1 alone (backend absence) would not either -- an honest "pending" state was always available. Both premises together are needed to explain why the specific choice was a fake "passed" signal rather than a visible "not yet built" one.

cause_insufficiency.reservation: Same joint-necessity reasoning as CLM-1, applied to the audit/replay hash badges.

conflict_claims.statement: D-1 and DP-1 cannot both be fully performed for the same indicator at the same time: an indicator is either a fixed literal (D-1) or conditioned on real backend state including an honest "pending" fallback (DP-1), never both.

alternative_candidates.rejected_because: RC-1 (the decision to render static mockups) is a real, evidenced cause of the current UDEs and is fully within this repository's control to fix for NC-1/NC-2 -- but treating RC-1 alone as *the* constraint would wrongly imply the whole gap is fixable by this repository alone, when NC-3 and NC-4 are gated on a backend service this repository does not own.

constraint_assessment.exploit_direction: Within this repo's control: ship IO-2 (real export gating) and IO-1 (a shared pending/local/backend-verified badge) immediately; explicitly render "pending backend integration" for NC-3/NC-4 rather than a fabricated pass, so the constraint (missing backend) becomes visible and trackable instead of hidden behind decoration.

goal_measure.name: Governance indicators backed by real computation or authorization
goal_measure.unit: of 4 tracked indicators (export gate, replay/audit hash, RBAC, redaction)

constraint_assessment.limiting_mechanism: Only NC-1 (export gating) and the shared badge component (IO-1) can be resolved entirely within this repository. NC-2 (replay hashing) is likely also resolvable client-side (a real digest over evidence/config can be computed before Praevisio exists), so it is a secondary opportunity. NC-3 (backend-authorized RBAC) and most of NC-4 (real redaction, which needs a policy/model boundary) cannot be fully satisfied without the oracular-backend/Praevisio services this repository does not own -- so the constraint limiting full governance-surface trustworthiness is the missing backend integration, not this frontend's engineering effort alone.

contradictions: DealGovernanceTab's integrity banner asserts "Replayable ✅" unconditionally, while its own runRegistry sample data (src/pages/Index.tsx lines 3305-3329) includes a row explicitly labeled "mismatch (toolchain drift)" -- the component's own sample data contradicts the badge it renders next to it.
coverage_gaps: The two vendored .docx files at the repo root ("Decision_Ops_Frontend_Visual_Guide_MVP (1).docx", "Decision_Ops_User_Stories_MVP (1).docx") were not parsed; they may contain additional authoritative product requirements beyond docs/*.txt and docs/*.org.
node_modules/, dist/, bun.lockb, and public/ static assets were excluded as vendored/build output, not project-authored logic.
src/components/ui/* (shadcn/radix primitive wrappers) and src/hooks/* were not individually inspected; they are largely vendored UI scaffolding rather than Oracular-specific business logic.

tests/bdd/steps/oracular.steps.js (2453 lines) was sampled by targeted grep for specific step definitions (redaction, RBAC-adjacent) rather than read in full; a full read might surface additional step logic relevant to this model.

Of the 40 files under features/, this model examined roughly a dozen directly relevant to the governance/epistemic-core distinction; the remaining feature files (deals_and_workspaces, monitoring_portfolio, work_queue_tasks, report_templates_admin, and others) were listed but not individually analyzed for this causal model.

Whatever backend(s) oracular-api / Praevisio / oracular-backend actually implement is outside this repository and was not inspected; CONSTRAINT-1 and AC-1 describe their absence from this frontend, not their real-world non-existence.

entities.reasoning: Directly stated across the project's own strategy documents rather than inferred from code: docs/one-pager.txt frames "Defensibility" as one of three named blockers ("'AI says so' doesn't survive an IC memo"), and docs/brand.txt states "Governance is not optional: if it can't survive scrutiny, it's not 'done.'" No live founder/user confirmation was available in this session, so this is marked corroborated (converging authored strategy docs), not user_confirmed.
entities.statement: Diligence outputs Oracular produces are defensible to a real IC panel and introduce no undisclosed governance risk.
Governance and audit-integrity signals shown to a reviewer (export gate, replay/hash badges, RBAC-gated views, redaction status) reflect the deal's real, computed state rather than a fixed display.
Competing explanations for a diligence question are evaluated without structural framing bias.
Sensitive evidence and governance-restricted actions are protected by boundaries a client cannot bypass on its own.

entities.satisfaction_criterion: The "Proceed to export" control is disabled whenever a required slot is unscored or a required claim's evidenceRefs is empty.
entities.statement: Export gating is computed from the deal's actual citation/scoring completeness, not a static display.

entities.satisfaction_criterion: evidenceHash/policyHash/resultHash values differ when the underlying evidence or config differ, and match only when they are actually equal (i.e. a real digest function feeds them).
entities.statement: Audit/replay hashes are computed from the run's real evidence, config, and output.

entities.reasoning: Marked influence: outside rather than control, because satisfying this fully requires a backend authorization service (owner_oracular_backend per features/ui_rbac_access_controls.feature) that does not exist inside this frontend repository; this repository can call such a service but cannot single-handedly build and operate it.
entities.satisfaction_criterion: hasGovernanceAccess (or its equivalent) is derived from a network response to an authenticated backend endpoint, not solely from window.localStorage or a URL parameter.
entities.statement: Access-control decisions are authorized by a backend the client cannot forge.

entities.reasoning: Marked influence: outside for the same reason as NC-3: real redaction requires a policy/model boundary (owner_praevisio_service per features/03_redaction_boundary.feature) not present in this repo.
entities.satisfaction_criterion: Evidence.redaction reflects a real content scan/mask, and masked text visibly differs from the raw source text.
entities.statement: Redaction actually transforms sensitive content before it is rendered or transmitted.

entities.satisfaction_criterion: buildHypothesis is the single constructor used for every hypothesis on a deal, with an identical {methodology, data, alignment, assumptions} rubric shape.
entities.statement: Every hypothesis, including residual H_NOA/H_UND hypotheses, is scored with the same rubric shape.

entities.satisfaction_criterion: createDealSeeded appends H_NOA and H_UND as two separate hypothesis records in open-world mode.
entities.statement: Missing-explanation (NOA) and missing-evidence (UND) uncertainty are represented as distinct hypotheses, never merged.
The Export Gate modal shows every governance checklist item as passed regardless of the current deal's actual citation and scoring completeness.
Audit-integrity and replay-status badges display fixed hash/status literals unrelated to the run's real evidence, config, or output.
Governance-restricted views and actions are shown or hidden based only on a role value the client itself set, with no backend authorization call in the path.
Evidence items are labeled with a redaction status determined only by evidence type, not by whether their content was actually masked.

The frontend's governance/compliance surface (export gate, audit/replay panel, RBAC gating, redaction labeling) was built as a static visual mockup of Praevisio's promised behavior -- fixed "passed" literals and role-only gating -- rather than wired to a real computation or authorization backend.
The backend services meant to back these checks (oracular-backend for auth/RBAC; Praevisio for redaction, replay hashing, and audit manifests) are not integrated into this frontend repository.

entities.reasoning: No ADR, changelog, or comment in this repository confirms intent either way; this is a plausible, but unconfirmed, charitable reading consistent with the owner_praevisio_service / owner_oracular_backend tags on the corresponding feature files (which presuppose a backend that will eventually exist). Recorded as an open_question rather than treated as settled.
entities.statement: The static, always-passing governance indicators were meant as a temporary placeholder for a later Praevisio/backend integration, not the deliberately intended final behavior.
Oracular's frontend credibly demonstrates a governance-conscious diligence product.

entities.reasoning: Evidenced by this repository's own provenance (EVD-8: Lovable/ gpt-engineer-app[bot] authorship, README "Welcome to your Lovable project"), which is consistent with a UI built to demonstrate the full product story to an audience before the backend exists.
entities.statement: The product demo/UI looks feature-complete enough to secure design partners/investors before the backend governance services exist.

entities.reasoning: Evidenced by the project's own stated brand principles (EVD-1, EVD-2: "Governance is not optional", "Defensibility... 'AI says so' doesn't survive an IC memo"), which make overstatement a named risk to the product's own value proposition, not just a generic quality concern.
entities.statement: Nothing shown to a reviewer overstates the product's actual current guarantees.
Render fixed "passed"/"applied"/"authorized" governance indicators immediately, independent of backend state.
Render a governance indicator only when backed by a real computation or authorization call, otherwise show an explicit "pending integration" state.
Design partners and investors will not commit without seeing the full intended workflow surface, not just the currently-real backend slice.
A reviewer who discovers a fabricated compliance signal (e.g. a fake replay hash) will discount the product's credibility more than they would from seeing an honest "not yet built" label.
There is no faster way to convey the full intended workflow than rendering it as if already true.
An honest "pending integration" fallback is achievable without materially hurting the demo narrative, if paired with a visible roadmap cue.

entities.reasoning: Targets ASM-4 (that only a fabricated "already true" display can convey the full workflow) by demonstrating a pending-but-visible state can still communicate the complete intended surface.
entities.statement: Adopt an explicit, honest third state for every governance/audit indicator -- "pending backend integration" -- distinct from pass/fail, and require every governance-adjacent UI element to render that state by default until a real computation or authorization call backs it.
Reviewers see an honest, three-state governance surface (pending / verified-locally / verified-by-backend) that still communicates the complete intended workflow.
Showing "pending integration" broadly might make the product look less finished to a fast-moving investor demo audience, undermining need B-1.

Pair every "pending integration" badge with a visible roadmap/target chip (e.g. "Governance: pending Praevisio integration") so the pending state reads as active, scoped progress rather than an abandoned promise.
There is no shared UI primitive today for a three-state (pending/local/backend-verified) governance indicator; every current indicator hardcodes a single visual state.
Determining "real" state per indicator (e.g. export completeness) requires each indicator's own data-derived boolean logic, which does not exist yet for any of the four indicators.

There is no backend endpoint yet to call for RBAC/redaction/replay verification (Praevisio/oracular-backend integration absent), so some indicators can only ever reach the "pending" state client-side.

entities.satisfaction_criterion: A single component renders all four governance indicators and takes an explicit state prop rather than each indicator inlining its own literal text.
entities.statement: A shared GovernanceStatus badge component exists, supporting pending/local/backend-verified states.

entities.satisfaction_criterion: "Citations present" and "All required slots scored" are booleans derived from currentDeal.hypotheses[].slots, not literals.
entities.statement: The export-gate checklist is computed from the current deal's real slot/evidence data.

entities.satisfaction_criterion: Each of the three indicators renders a labeled "pending integration" state rather than a fixed pass literal, whenever no backend call has actually succeeded.
entities.statement: RBAC/redaction/replay indicators explicitly render the "pending backend integration" state until Praevisio/oracular-backend endpoints exist.
The Export Gate modal's five checklist rows are hardcoded checkmarks; "Proceed to export" is disabled only by the local `exporting` flag.
A reviewer must not be able to proceed to export while a required claim actually lacks a citation or a required slot is actually unscored.
Compute each Export Gate checklist row (citations present, slots scored) from currentDeal's real slots/evidenceRefs, and disable "Proceed to export" whenever any required row is false.
Given a deal with at least one required slot whose evidenceRefs is empty, opening the Export Gate shows "Citations present" as failed and the "Proceed to export" button is disabled.

Existing demo walkthroughs or sales collateral may rely on the Export Gate always showing green; wiring real gating could surface an unexpected "blocked" state during a live demo of a deal that was never fully cited.

The absence of a real, wired backend (RBAC authorization, redaction, replay hashing via oracular-backend/Praevisio) currently limits how much of the governance surface this frontend repository alone can make honest.

evidence.interpretation: The product's own stated purpose ties G-1 directly to IC defensibility, not just feature completeness.
evidence.observation: "Defensibility: 'AI says so' doesn't survive an IC memo. If you can't show where a claim came from, you can't use it." listed as one of three named blockers to AI-assisted diligence adoption.

"Governance is not optional / If it can't survive scrutiny, it's not 'done.'" and "Determinism builds trust: Same evidence + config -> same outputs. Replay is a product feature." listed among six brand principles.

The Export Gate modal's five checklist rows ("All required slots scored", "Citations present", "No UNSCOPED roots", "Replayable run", "Injection guard") are static JSX text ending in a literal checkmark; "Proceed to export" is disabled only by the local `exporting` boolean. DealGovernanceTab elsewhere in the same file (lines 3288-3305) does compute claimsWithEvidence / missingPrimaryClaims from real slot data, showing the needed data exists but is unused by the gate.

The integrity banner ("Evidence manifest hash", "Config hash", "Replayable") and the run registry table are fixed JSX/array literals; the run registry's own sample data includes a row labeled "mismatch (toolchain drift)" even though the badge nearby reads "Replayable ✅" unconditionally. A repository-wide grep for sha256/crypto/createHash found only hardcoded display strings, no hashing function.

hasGovernanceAccess is a pure client-side role check; adminInviteGranted is set by matching a URL parameter against a single default token, then cached in localStorage; self-service signup can grant "admin" client-side; AuthUserRecord.password is stored and read back as plain text from localStorage. No network call authorizes any of this.
Evidence.redaction is assigned by `ev.type === "Link" ? "N/A" : "Applied"`, independent of content; "Redaction applied" banners are static text. No masking/content-scan function exists in src/.

The redaction, replay, and export-gate feature files carry an "@owner_praevisio_service" tag; the RBAC feature file carries "@owner_oracular_backend" instead. Both name a backend service outside this repository as the intended owner of the real check.

The decorative-governance pattern was introduced/extended across at least three separate commits ("add auth", "add governance integration", "Switched to Oracular API URL"), including one authored by "gpt-engineer-app[bot]" (a Lovable/AI website-builder bot); the project README itself opens with "Welcome to your Lovable project." This indicates a recurring, tool-assisted pattern across time rather than a single isolated slip.

buildHypothesis is the single constructor used for H1, H_ALT_1, H_ALT_2, and (in open-world mode) H_NOA/H_UND, each with an identical rubric shape; H_NOA and H_UND are two separate records, never merged.

predicted_effects.statement: If RC-1 + AC-1 is the true explanation, the same pattern (a governance indicator that always reads success/authorized regardless of real state) should recur across every other governance-adjacent surface in the frontend, not only the export gate.

additional_cause.reservation: OBS-2, OBS-3, and OBS-4 are plausibly independent contributing obstacles to H3d remaining open, not merely downstream consequences of RC-2.

cause_insufficiency.reservation: RC-2 alone blocks progress, but OBS-2/OBS-3/OBS-4 (no global-to-local state mapping, no factorized trace corpus, no serialized locality/coordination metrics) are additional missing conditions H3d also depends on; resolving RC-2 alone would not immediately resolve H3d.

causality_existence.reservation: UDE-4 (H3d remaining open) and CSF-2 not being satisfied are closely correlated by construction (both track Phase IV's incompleteness); whether UDE-4 independently causes CSF-2's unsatisfied status, versus both being joint downstream effects of RC-1/RC-2, is not independently established here.

cause_insufficiency.reservation: RC-1 and RC-2 more directly explain why CSF-2 is currently unsatisfied; whether UDE-4 contributes anything beyond restating their downstream consequence is unresolved.

tautology.reservation: Not clearly tautological, but overlaps substantially with RC-1's and RC-2's own causal claims into CSF-2-adjacent effects; needs disambiguation in a future scrutiny pass rather than being resolved here.

conflict_claims.statement: Exact global CUT reproduction (D-1) and restricting, coordinating, provenance-tagging, or redefining CUT whenever exact global separation is nonlocal (D-2) cannot both be fully satisfied by the same factorized CUT contract: satisfying D-1 in general requires nonlocal alternate-path knowledge that D-2's bounded-locality commitment rules out by construction.

alternative_candidates.rejected_because: RC-1 (flat Bell-table enumeration) explains the Phase III undesirable effects (UDE-1/UDE-2/UDE-3) and is a genuine root cause, but PHASE_IV.md's entire premise is to not scale the same flat cell; RC-1 is the historical root cause Phase IV is designed to bypass, not the presently limiting step for Phase IV's next output.

Rejected/deferred candidate goal (evidence EVD-5, EVD-6): "Deliver a superior monolithic FPGA PPL cell." The measured flat architecture is explicitly reported as not supporting H3c, so this alternative goal is not viable as currently evidenced. Dropped as a v1 candidate_goal entity during migration (v2 has no candidate-goal kind); recorded here rather than mis-typed as a cause.

Rejected/deferred candidate goal (evidence EVD-1): "Build a native physical ligature-memory device." Physical embodiment appears only as a later speculative roadmap phase (Phase VI); not viable as a near-term goal given no physical-device evidence exists. Dropped as a v1 candidate_goal entity during migration for the same reason as above.

Remaining unreviewed migration artifacts (not yet given real CLR review in this pass, unlike CLM-M6/CLM-M7 above): the "supports"/"risks"/"mitigates"/ "satisfies" links migrated to plain single-premise causal claims -- DE-4->CSF-2 (CLM-M13), DE-4->CSF-3 (CLM-M14), INJ-2->NBR-1 (CLM-M15), TRIM-1->NBR-1 (CLM-M16), INJ-1->NBR-2 (CLM-M17), TRIM-2->NBR-2 (CLM-M18), INJ-1->NC-2 (CLM-M19), ACT-2->CSF-3 (CLM-M27), ACT-4->CSF-3 (CLM-M29), UDE-3->CSF-3 (CLM-M30). These are directionally plausible (each named premise really does bear on its conclusion) but none has had CLR scrutiny applied; several (CLM-M14/M27/M29 all separately concluding CSF-3) may be better modeled as accumulating (`operator: magnitudinal`) partial contributions than as independently-sufficient single causes. Left as an honest standing scrutiny backlog (CLR-001 warnings on each) rather than resolved or fabricated in this pass.

Two backwards-directed migration artifacts were found and removed rather than left uncorrected: the migrated model briefly contained causal claims reading "INJ-2 causes RC-2" (from v1's "INJ-2 addresses RC-2"), "ACT-3 causes RC-1" (from v1's "ACT-3 mitigates RC-1"), and "RC-2 causes ACT-1" (from v1's "RC-2 addressed_by ACT-1"). All three inverted the real direction (an injection/action resolves a constraint/root-cause; it is not caused by it). They are now represented correctly through constraint_assessment.exploit_direction and the TR-1 transition instead of as causal claims.

additional_cause.reservation: Not confirmed whether imagine's retained vocabulary is unintentional drift or a deliberate choice (e.g. coach-facing copy may legitimately still say "commitment"); an independent product decision could also explain the divergence.

conflict_claims.statement: Consolidating into one shared module (D) and continuing to ship each surface independently without a blocking migration (D') cannot both be fully done at the same time: a real consolidation is itself a cross-cutting, blocking change to all three surfaces.

alternative_candidates.rejected_because: The imagine/keepable vocabulary drift (RC-3) is a symptom of missing reconciliation, not itself a throughput limiter -- teams can and do ship around it today. The missing acceptance gate is what makes any consolidation risky to attempt, which is the deeper limiter.

constraint_assessment.exploit_direction: Before any large consolidation, spend the smallest possible change wiring the two highest-value feature files (dynamic_path_loading, showing_up_adaptive_routing) to call the real src/lib/paths module, so the suite starts catching real regressions immediately.

goal_measure.name: features/*.feature scenarios whose step definitions import src/lib/paths or src/lib/keepable

constraint_assessment.limiting_mechanism: Because the acceptance suite cannot catch a regression in the real Path/Promise Loop mechanism, every change to src/lib/paths or src/lib/keepable relies solely on the vitest suite maintained by the same engineers making the change, with no independent, spec-traced gate. That risk throttles the team's willingness to attempt the Cloud's consolidation action (D), which is exactly the throughput-limiting mechanism standing between the current state and CSF-3, and transitively the confidence to keep extending CSF-1/CSF-2.

entities.statement: A Finder achieves real, honestly-reflected behavior change on their stated intention through a Guide-supported, adaptively-routed Promise Loop.
The Promise Loop mechanism -- recording action-promises and routing the next Loop stage from reflection evidence -- is real, deterministic, and demonstrably correct.
Visibility of Promise evidence is bounded by dose and layered by consent, so a Finder is never exposed beyond what they chose and no single reliability score is produced.
The platform's own model of itself -- its domain language, and the acceptance specifications describing it -- stays consistent with what is actually implemented, so people and agents can trust it.

conflict_claims.statement: Authoring more packets now (D-1) and deferring all authoring until the independent pipeline exists (D-2) cannot both be fully performed: packet-authoring effort is either applied now or deliberately withheld, not both.

The theory-dependency decision is an independent, parallel owner-decision track (roadmap.md, "in parallel") that does not block the adjudication/evidence critical path itself, per remediation- plan.md's sequencing section.

constraint_assessment.exploit_direction: Begin recruiting immediately, in parallel with every engineering track, per remediation-plan.md's own start order ("C1 recruiting the same day"), rather than sequencing it behind other work.

constraint_assessment.limiting_mechanism: Every downstream milestone toward the goal (blind re-adjudication, Gold Regions v2, challenge records, provenance, Phase-0 pilot activation, and the scorer-validity evaluation) requires input from an independent adjudicator or external packet author that does not yet exist in any recruited form. Engineering work on every other track (Track A, Track B, schemas, tooling) can and does proceed in parallel, but cannot substitute for this input, so throughput toward the goal is capped at the rate independent people are recruited and seated.

evidence.observation: Release-gate table lists independent adjudication and challenge records as "open"; non-certification reporting as "met"; README states the project's thesis and its "strongest warranted self-claim" (R1 reliance).

All three adjudicator seats and the external packet-author seat read "open". "No independent adjudicator or external packet author is represented as confirmed until a dated written confirmation is appended here." Every one of Manual §4.2's seven duties (source selection, drafting, challenge, adjudication, hidden-set stewardship, scorer implementation, candidate-method development) is currently held by the originating research program.

Precommitted scorer-validity protocol (per-dimension Spearman rho >= 0.7; banded agreement >= 80%; adversarial fixtures 1/3/4/5; length/ template R^2 <= 0.5) is written and preregistered (STUDY-SCORER-VALIDITY, preregistered=true), but its epistemic-status line states "the scorer's validity has not yet been evaluated against it."
Phase-0 pilot design is drafted but not activated. CAP-PILOT-ACTIVATION and CAP-RUN-MANIFEST are "specified"; all three scenarios in features/studies/phase0_activation.feature are skipped (@wip).

The reporting linter's PROHIBITED_PATTERNS enforce non-certification language. `python -m uptakebench lint-report --prohibited-only README.md docs/*.md` passed (PASS on every file) when run during this analysis at commit eb075f5877e0a1f8f64955f576e84478fdc03277.

"Owner decision required before any public v0.1 claim. Status: open." Two options (publish the Signs of Change manuscripts as preprints, or formally decouple) are stated; neither has been selected or logged.

Deterministic evidence-anchoring rules are implemented; both acceptance scenarios pass (verified during this analysis: `python -m behave` shows CAP-ANCHOR-VERIFICATION's 2 scenarios passed, 0 failed).

`python -m pytest -q` run during this analysis: 24 passed, 2 xfailed overall, matching the documented CI policy that adversarial fixtures 1/3/4/5 pass and fixtures 2/6 remain expected failures (xfail) pending the adjudicated layer.

Decision rule: "Any criterion failed or unevaluated: the lexical layer remains provisional ... HYP-1 claims remain barred." No pilot data exists yet against which to evaluate the four precommitted criteria.
"the same source currently supplies theory, packets, Gold Regions, scorer, and interpretation — the exact author-diversity/double-role defect classes ... the benchmark exists to detect."

Every one of the 8 hypotheses carries conclusion "not_tested"; evidence_maturity is capped at "mechanism" (HYP-1, HYP-2, HYP-6) or lower ("design"/"none") for the rest, despite capability statuses of "implemented" or "partial" for the mechanisms that are built.
"Phase 1 = UptakeBench v0.1 proper (24 packets, 16 held out, external authors, baselines) ... The shipped package is Phase 0 material regardless of its 'v0.1/Alpha' labels."
"Do not author new packets or build the 24-packet corpus yet ... Packet production restarts only after G3 with external authors (C1) and the C3 provenance machinery."
"Authoring more packets before the challenge/provenance process is operational" is listed among deliberately deferred work, distinct from "More lexical scorer heuristics" and other deferrals.

open_questions: Whether confirming adjudicators actually leads to a completed, sufficiently reliable C2 blind re-adjudication (CLM-5's causality_ existence is marked "open") is untested; recruiting could succeed while reliability still falls short of the exploratory alpha target (NBR-2).

Whether resolving the theory-dependency decision (INJ-2 / CSF-5) has any bearing on the independent-evidence critical path (CSF-1 / CSF-2 / CSF-3) beyond citation practice is treated here as independent tracks per remediation-plan.md's own sequencing (ASM-6), but that separation itself has not been independently verified.

Whether 8 packets, balanced 4 defective / 2 clean / 2 ambiguous, is enough to produce interpretable reliability statistics in the Phase-0 pilot, or whether the Cloud's need for Phase-1 scale (N-1) will surface as a blocker even after CSF-1 is met, is not addressed by this model.

predicted_effects.statement: If RC-1 (no independent panel or author recruited) is genuinely why single-source concentration and untested hypotheses persist, then even partial recruitment (e.g. confirming one adjudicator before the full C2 tooling lands) should visibly begin unblocking at least some packets toward independent re-adjudication.

If RC-2 (the unresolved theory-dependency decision) is genuinely why public construct claims stay restricted, rather than the independent- evidence gap already covered by RC-1, then resolving RC-2 alone (without any new empirical evidence) should be sufficient to lift that specific restriction, leaving the R1 bound on public model-ranking or certification claims unchanged (those are gated by CSF-1/CSF-2/CSF-3, not by RC-2).
